type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The anaerobic digestion of wastewater sludges produces a Biogas that contains Methane (62.0%), Carbon Dioxide (37.5%), H2S (0.4%) and other trace gases. At Gold Bar the Biogas is used as the fuel burned in the plant boilers which heat the entire facility and provide heat for the Digestors themselves. Excess biogas is flared. Since the Biogas contains H2S sulfur dioxide is emitted during combustion.

Biosolids produced at the Gold Bar Wastewater Treatment Plant are pumped to the Clover Bar Biosolids Recycling Facility where, after gravity thickening, they are either applied to surrounding farmland as soil conditioner or combined with municipal solid waste to produce commercial grade compost.

substance.en: Nitrate ion in solution at pH >= 6.0
substance.fr: Nitrate (ion en sol. à un pH de >= 6.0)

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: As a direct result of the process used to reduce the releases of Ammonia to the North Saskatchewan River nitrate releases are increased. The products of the nitrification of Ammonia are nitrite and nitrate.

The Biological Nutrient Removal process utilized at the Gold Bar Wastewater Treatment Plant removes Phosphorus from the wastewater flow leading to significantly reduced levels of phosphorus entering the North Saskatchewan River. The phosphorus levels of the biosolids that are produced are increased resulting in a high value nutrient product that is applied to local farmland and used in the production of compost.

comments.comment: Variations in both the total volumes of effluent discharged at outfalls and CSO (combined sewer overflows) and the average concentrations of each parameter at those locations.  The average concentration of each parameter at these outfall and CSO locations is somewhat dependent on the variations of the average concentrations of each parameter of the incoming influent.
Changes in production levels
